title: cerastium holosteoides subsp. vulgare
intro: Cerastium holosteoides subsp. vulgare, also known as the common mouse-ear chickweed, is a subspecies of the Cerastium holosteoides plant, a type of flowering plant in the carnation family that grows in various parts of the world. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Cerastium holosteoides subsp. vulgare include its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and act as a diuretic, as well as its ability to help manage digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of Cerastium holosteoides subsp. vulgare can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
